author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
title: The Passage in Romans.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: Now carefully consider this entire passage, and see whether it says anything about circumcision, or the Sabbath, or anything else pertaining to a foreshadowing sacrament.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: Does not its whole scope amount to this, that the letter which forbids sin fails to give man life, but rather “killeth,” by increasing concupiscence, and aggravating sinfulness by transgression, unless indeed grace liberates us by the law of faith, which is in Christ Jesus, when His love is “shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost, which is given to us?”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: The apostle having used these words: “That we should serve in newness of spirit, and not in the oldness of the letter,” goes on to inquire, “What shall we say then?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: Is the law sin?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: Nay; I had not known sin, but by the law: for I had not known lust, except the law had said, Thou shalt not covet.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: But sin, taking occasion by the commandment, wrought in me all manner of concupiscence.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: For without the law sin was dead.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: For I was alive without the law once; but when the commandment came, sin revived, and I died.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: And the commandment, which was ordained to life, I found to be unto death.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: For sin, taking occasion by the commandment deceived me, and by it slew me.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: Wherefore the law is holy, and the commandment holy, and just, and good.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: Was then that which is good made death unto me?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: But sin, that it might appear sin, worked death in me by that which is good; that sin by the commandment might become exceeding sinful.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: For we know that the law is spiritual; whereas I am carnal, sold under sin.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: For that which I do I allow not: for what I would, that I do not; but what I hate, that I do.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: If then I do that which I would not, I consent unto the law that it is good.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: But then it is no longer I that do it, but sin that dwelleth in me.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: For I know that in me (that is, in my flesh) dwelleth no good thing.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: To will, indeed, is present with me; but how to perform that which is good I find not.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: For the good that I would, I do not; but the evil which I would not, that I do.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: Now, if I do that which I would not, it is no more I that do it, but sin that dwelleth in me.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: I find then a law, that, when I would do good, evil is present with me.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: For I delight in the law of God after the inward man: but I see another law in my members warring against the law of my mind, and bringing me into captivity to the law of sin which is in my members.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: O wretched man that I am! who shall deliver me from the body of this death?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: The grace of God, through Jesus Christ out Lord.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: So then with the mind I myself serve the law of God, but with the flesh the law of sin.”
